description: ring is marked optional behind 'dnssec' feature; if a downstream build disables the dnssec feature, DNSSEC validation is silently compiled out, weakening trust in DNS responses.
Cargo.toml depends on unmaintained webpki 0.22 (deprecated since 2023, superseded by rustls-webpki); enables outdated cert-validation logic when 'dot' or 'doh' features enabled.
Default DnsConfig sets dnssec_enabled=false; out-of-the-box deployments accept unsigned DNS responses and provide no protection against DNS spoofing without explicit opt-in.
Default DnsConfig sets bogus_priv=false; reverse lookups for RFC 1918 addresses are forwarded to upstream resolvers, leaking internal network topology.
Default DnsConfig sets stop_dns_rebind=false; DNS rebinding protection is disabled by default, leaving routers/IoT devices behind the resolver vulnerable to web-origin attacks.
Default TftpConfig sets tftp_secure=false; secure-mode ownership checks in check_tftp_fileperm() are bypassed, allowing any world-readable file under tftp root to be served.
DNSSEC_LIMIT_NSEC3_ITERS=500 far exceeds RFC 9276's recommendation of 0; high iteration NSEC3 records can be used to mount CPU-DoS attacks against the validator.
Hardcoded default CHGRP="dip" grants the daemon membership in the dial-up/PPP group, conferring unneeded device/network privileges that violate least-privilege.
write_leases() uses a predictable temp filename (lease_file_path.with_extension("tmp")) and OpenOptions without mode(0o600); lease file with MACs/hostnames is world-readable by default umask.
execute_lease_script #[cfg(unix)] block documents a TODO for privilege dropping; lease-change scripts therefore run with dnsmasq's full daemon privileges, breaking helper isolation.
On timeout, code comments that child was moved into wait_with_output and so cannot be killed; the runaway script continues until OS cleanup, allowing per-event resource exhaustion.
handle_decline marks any client-requested IP as declined for an hour without authentication; an attacker on the network can iteratively exhaust the entire DHCPv4 pool.
handle_decline hardcodes Duration::from_secs(3600) instead of using constants::DECLINE_BACKOFF (=600); inconsistent backoff prolongs IP-pool starvation versus documented behavior.
handle_release frees the lease for ciaddr without verifying that the lease's recorded client MAC matches request.client_hardware_addr(); any LAN attacker can release others' leases.
Numerous eprintln!('[CACHE DEBUG]'...) and '[INSERT]' debug statements emit cache contents, eviction events, and HashMap sizes to stderr in release builds.
verify_rsa accepts DNSSEC algorithms 5 and 7 (RSA/SHA-1, RSASHA1-NSEC3-SHA1) via ring's *_SHA1_FOR_LEGACY_USE_ONLY API; SHA-1 is broken for collision resistance.
verify_rsa uses RSA_PKCS1_1024_8192_SHA256_FOR_LEGACY_USE_ONLY which permits 1024-bit RSA keys; weak DNSSEC keys (below 2048 bits) are accepted as valid signers.
is_private_or_reserved_ip(): incomplete deny-list — does not unwrap IPv4-mapped IPv6 (::ffff:a.b.c.d), so rebinding to private IPv4 via mapped form bypasses stop-dns-rebind protection.
is_private_or_reserved_ip(): missing CGN/Shared Address Space 100.64.0.0/10 (RFC 6598); rebinding to carrier-grade-NAT addresses is not blocked by stop-dns-rebind.
is_private_or_reserved_ip(): documented decision to NOT block TEST-NET ranges (192.0.2.0/24, 198.51.100.0/24, 203.0.113.0/24); attacker-controlled DNS can rebind to these reserved ranges.
wait_for_upstream_response recv_from on unconnected UDP socket without verifying upstream_addr matches queried server; spoofed responses guessing port+id are accepted.
Multiple eprintln!('[FORWARDER DEBUG]...') statements in production code path leak query state and cache internals to stderr regardless of configured log level.
DNS rebinding filter only iterates response_message.answers; A/AAAA records placed by attacker in authority or additional sections are not checked against is_private_or_reserved_ip().
compress_name() emits per-label eprintln! debug output to stderr in production builds, leaking DNS query/response names and internal compression-map state to anyone reading stderr.
from_bytes uses Vec::with_capacity(header.qdcount/ancount/nscount/arcount as usize) sourced from attacker-controlled u16 fields; each malformed packet may pre-allocate up to ~5MB before parse-fail.
drop_privileges_safely() only invokes privilege drop when current_uid.is_root(); a process started non-root with CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE retains all granted capabilities for the daemon's lifetime.
build_nft_command interpolates the operator-supplied set_spec directly into the nft command buffer with format!; a malicious config can inject arbitrary nftables statements (rules, deletes).
table.pfrt_name[..name_bytes.len()].copy_from_slice(name_bytes) panics when name_bytes.len() exceeds pfrt_name (32 bytes); a long table_name aborts the firewall worker thread.
Linux drop_privileges() silently falls back to user's primary group on getgrnam failure, only emitting a warn!; misconfigured group is masked, dnsmasq runs with unintended group identity.
Linux drop_privileges() decides 'in_container' from spoofable filesystem markers (/.dockerenv, /proc/1/cgroup strings); creating /.dockerenv before exec disables setuid/setgid entirely.
fd_to_tcp_listener calls StdTcpListener::from_raw_fd(fd) without verifying via fstat that the fd is a SOCK_STREAM listening socket; systemd socket-activation type mismatches yield UB.
fd_to_udp_socket calls StdUdpSocket::from_raw_fd(fd) without verifying via fstat that the fd is a SOCK_DGRAM socket; systemd socket-activation type mismatches yield UB.
wrap_raw_fd_udp accepts a RawFd and calls UdpSocket::from_raw_fd without fstat-verifying it is actually an AF_INET/AF_INET6 SOCK_DGRAM; a wrong-type fd produces undefined socket behavior.
check_tftp_fileperm canonicalize→metadata→ownership-check→File::open is non-atomic; a symlink swap in the window between metadata() and open() may serve a file outside the tftp root.
execute_shell_event documents that tokio::process::Command lacks setuid; lease/tftp/arp/relay scripts therefore inherit the dnsmasq daemon's privileges instead of being de-privileged.
build_environment inserts DNSMASQ_SUPPLIED_HOSTNAME, DNSMASQ_VENDOR_CLASS, DNSMASQ_USER_CLASS raw from client packet without sanitizing newlines/NUL/control chars before exec into helper script env.
log_message truncates by byte index (message[..MAX_MESSAGE-3].to_string()) which panics if MAX_MESSAGE-3 lands mid UTF-8 codepoint; attacker can craft log content to abort daemon.
